items.title: Sync Preview / Dry Run Mode
items.description: Before committing a sync, show users a detailed diff of exactly what would change in each target harness — new files, modified sections, deleted entries. Users are afraid to sync because they don't know what they'll break. A /sync --preview command removes that fear and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Feature Parity Heatmap
items.description: A visual table (rendered in terminal with color) showing every Claude Code feature (skills, MCP servers, rules, agents, commands) and which target harnesses support it natively, partially, or not at all. Users waste time discovering that a feature silently didn't sync — this surfaces that up front so they can make informed decisions about which harnesses to invest in.

items.title: Config Drift Notifications
items.description: Detect when target harness configs have been manually edited and drift from the expected synced state. Surface a warning the next time the user opens Claude Code or runs any /sync command. Solves the silent drift problem — users edit Cursor rules directly, forget about it, then wonder why behavior is inconsistent across tools.

items.title: Full Config Bundle Export/Import
items.description: Export an entire HarnessSync config bundle (source + all target states + override layers) as a single portable archive. Import it on a new machine to get identical harness setup in seconds. Solves new machine setup pain — currently developers spend hours recreating their AI harness environment from scratch.

items.title: Project-Type Adaptive Sync
items.description: Auto-detect project type (Python/Node/Go/Rust/etc.) from the codebase and apply relevant config profiles — only syncing skills, rules, and MCP servers that are relevant to that stack. A Python ML project doesn't need frontend rules cluttering the AI context in every harness. Reduces noise and makes AI assistants more focused per-project.

items.title: Auto-Sync on Config Save
items.description: Watch for writes to CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules/, .mcp.json, or settings.json and trigger incremental sync automatically without user intervention. Currently users forget to run /sync after making config changes, causing harnesses to silently lag behind. This closes the feedback loop so every config change is instantly reflected everywhere.

items.title: Secrets Leak Prevention
items.description: Scan config files before syncing for patterns that look like API keys, tokens, passwords, or other secrets. Block the sync and warn the user before writing sensitive data into Cursor rules, Aider conventions, or Gemini settings files. Prevents accidental credential exposure — a real risk when configs evolve organically and users copy-paste things into CLAUDE.md.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: Support a 'team base config' sourced from a shared git repo or URL, merged with personal overrides. Teams standardizing on Claude Code can publish a base CLAUDE.md + rules/MCP setup and every developer's HarnessSync pulls it automatically. Solves the team onboarding problem — new developers currently have to manually copy team AI conventions.

items.title: MCP Server Discovery & Recommendations
items.description: Connect to public MCP server registries (e.g., mcp.run, GitHub topics) to suggest popular MCP servers relevant to the current project's tech stack. Users don't know what MCP servers exist or are compatible — this surfaces high-value servers they're missing, making Claude Code (and all synced harnesses) more capable with minimal effort.

items.title: Sync History Time Machine
items.description: Maintain a timestamped history of every sync operation with the before/after state of each target. Users can browse history and restore any target to any previous state. Solves 'I synced something and now Cursor is broken' — currently users have no easy way to undo a sync that introduced a regression.

items.title: Named Sync Profiles (Work/Personal/Project)
items.description: Define multiple named profiles (e.g., 'work', 'personal', 'oss-contrib') each with different source configs, target selections, and overrides. Switch profiles with /sync --profile work. Solves context switching — AI developers often work across employer projects, personal projects, and open source, each needing different rules, tools, and permissions synced.

items.title: Third-Party Adapter Plugin SDK
items.description: Define a stable adapter interface and publish a mini-SDK so the community can write adapters for new harnesses (Cline, Continue, Aider variants, etc.) without waiting for official support. HarnessSync's value is proportional to the number of harnesses it covers — a plugin SDK grows coverage exponentially through community contribution.

items.title: Sync Webhooks & External Triggers
items.description: Fire webhooks on sync events (sync-complete, drift-detected, sync-failed) to integrate with Slack, CI pipelines, or custom tooling. Teams want to know when AI harness configs change across the team — this enables audit trails, notifications, and automated validation pipelines without any in-plugin UI.

items.title: Natural Language → Config Generation
items.description: Accept a plain-English description ('I want all harnesses to avoid suggesting console.log and always use structured logging') and generate the appropriate rules, translated and adapted for each target harness. Lowers the barrier for non-experts to configure AI coding assistants — most developers know what behavior they want but not the config format to express it.

items.title: Sync Coverage Score
items.description: After every sync, show a per-harness coverage score: what % of your Claude Code capabilities (skills, MCP tools, rules, agents) are reflected in each target. 'Codex: 73% coverage — 4 skills unsupported, 2 MCP servers incompatible.' Makes the value of HarnessSync tangible and helps users prioritize which gaps to close.

items.title: Conflict Resolution Wizard
items.description: When a target config has been manually modified since the last sync, present an interactive merge UI showing source vs. target vs. merged result for each conflicting section. Let users choose 'keep mine', 'take synced', or 'merge both'. Prevents the current blunt overwrite behavior that destroys intentional per-harness customizations.

items.title: Sync Analytics & Insights
items.description: Track and display sync metrics over time: most-changed files, sync frequency per harness, error rates, most-unsupported features. Surfaces patterns like 'you sync Codex 10x more than Gemini — is Gemini still needed?' or 'this MCP server has failed to sync 8 times'. Helps users audit and optimize their multi-harness setup.

items.title: Custom Config Lint Rules
items.description: Allow users and teams to define custom lint rules for their AI configs (e.g., 'every rule must have a rationale comment', 'MCP servers must specify allowed tools', 'CLAUDE.md must have a Testing section'). Runs on /sync-lint. Enforces team standards for AI config quality — currently there's no way to ensure configs meet organizational conventions.

items.title: Harness Version Compatibility Checker
items.description: Detect installed versions of each target harness CLI and warn when synced configs use features not yet supported by that version. 'Gemini CLI 0.3 doesn't support multi-agent configs — your agents/ folder will be ignored.' Prevents silent failures where configs sync correctly but are ignored by an older harness version.

items.title: Cross-Harness Config Search
items.description: Search across all synced harness configs simultaneously for a keyword, rule ID, MCP server name, or skill name. '/sync-search postgres' shows every place postgres is mentioned across Codex/Gemini/Cursor/etc. configs. Saves time when debugging inconsistent AI behavior — users currently have to manually grep multiple directories.

items.title: Interactive Onboarding Wizard
items.description: A guided /sync-setup wizard that auto-detects installed harnesses, shows what it found, asks which ones to sync, explains what each sync will do, and runs the first sync with a preview. First-time users are overwhelmed by options and unsure which harnesses are installed or what to sync. A wizard reduces setup from 20 minutes of doc-reading to 2 minutes of guided clicks.

items.title: Multi-Machine Sync via Git
items.description: Use a git repo as the sync backend — push Claude Code config state to a designated branch, pull it on other machines, and trigger local sync. A developer with a work laptop, home desktop, and cloud VM currently has to maintain harness configs on each machine separately. Git-backed sync makes multi-machine setup seamless.

items.title: Rule Rationale Annotations
items.description: Allow rules in CLAUDE.md to be tagged with metadata (rationale, severity, owner, last-updated) that gets stripped from the synced output but preserved in source. Creates a layer of institutional knowledge around why each rule exists, without polluting target configs with Claude-specific comments. Solves the 'why does this rule exist?' problem that plagues long-lived codebases.

items.title: Continuous MCP Server Health Monitor
items.description: Periodically ping configured MCP servers and alert users when one becomes unreachable or returns errors. Dead MCP servers silently degrade AI assistant capability — users often only discover an MCP server is down when they need it and get confused behavior. Proactive monitoring surfaces this before it causes productivity loss.

items.title: Skill & Rule Usage Analytics
items.description: Track which skills, rules, and agents are actually invoked across harnesses and surface the least-used ones as candidates for pruning. AI configs accumulate cruft over time — skills added for a one-time task, rules that are now contradictory, MCP servers never actually used. Usage data makes config hygiene actionable instead of guesswork.

items.title: Conditional / Trigger-Based Sync Rules
items.description: Define conditions for when specific config sections sync to specific targets: 'only sync database MCP servers to harnesses running in repos with a /migrations folder' or 'sync security rules only to work-profile targets'. Gives power users fine-grained control without sacrificing the default 'sync everything everywhere' simplicity.

items.title: Community Config Template Library
items.description: A curated library of shareable CLAUDE.md + rules templates for common roles and stacks (React dev, ML researcher, DevOps engineer, security auditor). Users can /sync-template apply react-developer to seed their Claude Code config with community best practices, then sync to all harnesses. Solves the blank-canvas problem — most users don't know what good AI config looks like.

items.title: Sync Impact Predictor
items.description: Before syncing, analyze pending changes and predict their likely behavioral impact: 'Adding this rule may conflict with Codex's built-in code style preferences' or 'This MCP server adds 3 new tools that will appear in Gemini's tool suggestions'. Uses pattern matching and known harness behaviors. Helps users understand the downstream consequences of config changes before they apply.

items.title: AI Behavior Regression Detection
items.description: After sync, run a lightweight set of standard prompts against each harness and compare outputs to a baseline. Flag when a config change causes a harness to behave significantly differently. Solves the 'I synced my config and now Codex gives weird suggestions' problem — currently users have no systematic way to detect config-induced regressions.

items.title: Config Size Optimizer & Deduplicator
items.description: Analyze CLAUDE.md and rules files to detect duplicate rules, redundant instructions, conflicting directives, and sections that are too long for context windows. Suggest consolidations and flag bloat before it degrades AI quality across all synced harnesses. Large configs silently hurt AI quality — most users have no visibility into this.

items.title: Auto-Generated Sync Changelog
items.description: Maintain a human-readable SYNC_CHANGELOG.md that auto-documents every config change with timestamp, what changed, and which harnesses were affected. Useful for teams doing retrospectives on AI behavior changes or debugging when a workflow regressed. Answers 'when did my AI coding assistant start refusing to use async/await?' with a definitive history.

items.title: Harness API Deprecation Warnings
items.description: Track known deprecated config fields and formats across harnesses (e.g., Codex dropping support for a config key, Gemini changing agent schema) and warn users proactively when their synced config uses deprecated features. Prevents 'silent breakage' when harnesses update their config format and existing synced configs silently stop working.

items.title: Interactive Scope Selector for Partial Sync
items.description: An interactive TUI (terminal UI) that lets users select exactly which sections to sync in a given run: checkboxes for rules, skills, MCP servers, agents per target harness. Solves 'I only want to sync my new MCP server to Cursor, not re-sync everything else' — a common need that the current CLI flags don't cover elegantly.

items.title: Config Health Score with Actionable Recommendations
items.description: Score each harness config on dimensions like completeness, freshness, size efficiency, and conflict-freedom. Output a concrete list of recommended actions ranked by impact: 'Add a Testing section to improve Gemini context quality (+12 points)'. Makes HarnessSync a proactive advisor, not just a copy tool — turns passive sync into active config improvement.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.
